user_query:
I’m organizing a weekly Nora Payne fan club sync and want a small, self-contained scheduled job that helps me prep. Please inspect the provided input/schedule.json and scripts/run_tasks.py, then implement and schedule a new job named "fetch_official_homepage" that runs daily at 09:00 local time. Requirements:

1) Internet download and extraction
- The job must download the homepage HTML from Nora Payne’s official website. Do not hardcode a URL in code; instead, read two fields from the job’s config in input/schedule.json: "source_domain" and "page_path". Construct the request as scheme "https" with those two fields.
- For now, set source_domain to "example.com" and page_path to "/" in input/schedule.json as a safe default I will later change to Nora Payne’s real official site. The code must work generically for any domain/path provided via the config.
- Save the raw HTML to workspace/data/homepage_raw.html. Extract the <title> text and the <meta name="description"> content. Write a structured JSON record to workspace/data/homepage_meta.json with these fields: {"source_domain", "page_path", "fetched_at" (ISO 8601), "status_code" (HTTP), "page_title", "meta_description", "content_sha256" (SHA-256 of the raw HTML)}.
- Append a log line for every run to workspace/logs/fetch.log with at least timestamp, source_domain, status_code, and bytes_downloaded.
- Handle network errors gracefully: write status_code if available (or "N/A"), still emit homepage_meta.json with nulls for missing fields, and log the failure.

2) Scheduling and code/config inspection
- Update input/schedule.json to include the new job with name "fetch_official_homepage", type "fetch_official_homepage", schedule "daily 09:00", and a config object containing {"source_domain": "example.com", "page_path": "/"}. Do not remove the existing "example_echo" job.
- Implement the job in scripts/run_tasks.py by following the existing pattern in the file (dispatch by job type). Keep the solution cross-platform and rely only on the Python standard library. Do not use OS-specific schedulers; the schedule specification should live in the JSON config and be runnable via the provided script.
- Provide a way to run this job immediately for verification using: python scripts/run_tasks.py --run-once fetch_official_homepage.

3) Prepare meeting notes/action items
- Using the template at input/agenda_template.md, generate a filled-out notes file at workspace/notes/meeting_notes.md. Replace placeholders with values from workspace/data/homepage_meta.json. The notes must include these values visibly: source_domain, fetched_at, status_code, page_title, and meta_description. Also include at least three concrete action items relevant to a Nora Payne fan club meeting (for example, checking for new tour or merch announcements, sharing favorite lyrics, or curating a listening list). Feel free to auto-generate sensible action items based on whether meta_description/text is present.

Deliverables to produce or modify:
- input/schedule.json (updated with the new job; keep the existing job intact)
- workspace/data/homepage_raw.html
- workspace/data/homepage_meta.json
- workspace/notes/meeting_notes.md (based on input/agenda_template.md)
- workspace/logs/fetch.log

Verification:
- I will run: python scripts/run_tasks.py --run-once fetch_official_homepage and check that the files above exist with the described fields/sections.
- Ensure the code is robust if the homepage has no <meta name="description">; meta_description should be null or empty in that case, and this should still be reflected in the notes.
- No external dependencies, credentials, or OS-specific schedulers. Keep all paths exactly as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
